meta.name: DOCS Dimensional Obsessive-Compulsive Scale
meta.description: The DOCS is a 20-item self-report scale that assesses obsessive-compulsive symptom severity across four dimensions: contamination, responsibility for harm, unacceptable thoughts, and symmetry/ordering.
meta.author: Abramowitz JS, Deacon BJ, Olatunji BO, et al. (2010)
meta.reference: Abramowitz JS, Deacon BJ, Olatunji BO, et al. Assessment of obsessive-compulsive symptom dimensions. Psychol Assess. 2010;22(1):180-198.
meta.instruction: The following questions ask about four different types of concerns. Rate the degree to which you have experienced each aspect over the past month.

questions.text: How much time have you spent thinking about contamination (dirt, germs, chemicals) or feeling contaminated?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent contamination concerns?
If you had contamination thoughts but could not wash/clean, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by contamination concerns and washing/cleaning?
How difficult has it been to disregard contamination thoughts and refrain from washing/cleaning?
How much time have you spent thinking you might be responsible for something terrible happening?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent being responsible for harm?
If unable to check or get reassurance about potential harm, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by harm thoughts and checking/reassurance-seeking?
How difficult has it been to disregard harm-responsibility thoughts and refrain from checking?
How much time have you spent on unwanted intrusive thoughts (violent, sexual, immoral)?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent unwanted intrusive thoughts?
When you had unacceptable thoughts and could not perform a ritual, how distressed did you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by unacceptable thoughts and related rituals?
How difficult has it been to disregard unacceptable thoughts and refrain from rituals?
How much time have you spent on the need for symmetry, order, or balance?
To what extent have you avoided situations to prevent symmetry/order concerns?
If unable to arrange things to achieve symmetry, how distressed would you become?
To what extent has your daily routine been disrupted by symmetry concerns and ordering?
How difficult has it been to disregard symmetry thoughts and refrain from ordering?

ranges.label: Minimal OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Your total score falls within the minimal range, indicating that obsessive-compulsive symptoms are not a significant concern at this time. You may experience occasional intrusive thoughts or mild preferences for order, but these do not cause meaningful distress or interfere with daily functioning.
suggestions: No clinical concern is indicated at this time; continue maintaining your current healthy thought patterns and routines
Be aware that occasional intrusive thoughts are a normal human experience and do not require special intervention
If symptoms increase during stressful periods, consider re-taking this assessment to monitor any changes

ranges.label: Mild OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates mild obsessive-compulsive symptoms that may cause noticeable distress but remain largely manageable. You might spend some time on intrusive thoughts, engage in occasional compulsive behaviors, or avoid certain situations, though your overall daily functioning is mostly preserved.
suggestions: Learn about the nature of OCD and how intrusive thoughts differ from intentions; understanding this distinction can reduce distress
Practice accepting unwanted thoughts without engaging in rituals or mental compulsions to neutralize them
Consider evidence-based CBT self-help workbooks focused on OCD, such as those using Exposure and Response Prevention principles
Review your subscale scores to identify which OCD dimension is most prominent and target your coping strategies accordingly

ranges.label: Moderate OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate obsessive-compulsive symptoms that are likely interfering with your daily functioning, relationships, or work productivity. You may spend considerable time on obsessions and compulsions, experience significant distress when unable to perform rituals, and avoid multiple situations.
suggestions: Seek evaluation from a mental health professional who specializes in OCD, as symptoms at this level typically benefit from treatment
Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) is the gold-standard psychotherapy for OCD and has strong evidence for effectiveness
Review your subscale scores to identify which OCD dimensions are most problematic and discuss these with your treatment provider
Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs) may be recommended as part of your treatment plan if therapy alone is insufficient
Join an OCD support group to connect with others who understand your experience and can share effective coping strategies

ranges.label: Severe OCD Symptoms
ranges.description: Your score indicates severe obsessive-compulsive symptoms causing significant impairment across multiple life domains including work, relationships, and self-care. You likely spend extensive time on obsessions and compulsions, experience intense distress, and may have substantially restricted your daily activities.
suggestions: Please seek professional treatment as soon as possible; severe OCD is a highly treatable condition with proper intervention
A combination of ERP therapy and medication (typically SSRIs at higher doses) is often the most effective approach for severe OCD
OCD is a neurobiological condition, not a character flaw; effective evidence-based treatments can significantly reduce symptom severity
If current treatments have been insufficient, ask your provider about augmentation strategies or intensive outpatient OCD programs
Involve supportive family members or partners in your treatment, as family accommodation of OCD can inadvertently maintain symptoms

contamination.label: Minimal
contamination.description: You report no significant contamination-related concerns. Routine hygiene practices are appropriate without excessive worry about dirt, germs, or chemicals. No intervention is needed for this dimension.

contamination.label: Mild
contamination.description: You experience mild contamination-related obsessions or compulsions, such as occasional excessive handwashing or discomfort with perceived contaminants. These concerns cause some distress but do not substantially interfere with daily activities.

contamination.label: Moderate
contamination.description: Contamination concerns are moderately affecting your daily life, potentially leading to time-consuming washing rituals, avoidance of public spaces, or difficulty touching commonly shared objects. Professional guidance using ERP techniques is recommended.

contamination.label: Severe
contamination.description: Severe contamination obsessions and washing compulsions are dominating your daily routine, causing extensive avoidance and significant functional impairment. You may spend hours on decontamination rituals. Specialized OCD treatment is strongly recommended.

responsibility.label: Minimal
responsibility.description: You report no significant concerns about being responsible for harm or making catastrophic mistakes. Normal caution and checking behaviors are present without excessive worry or compulsive reassurance seeking.

responsibility.label: Mild
responsibility.description: You experience mild checking or reassurance-seeking behaviors driven by concerns about causing harm or being responsible for negative outcomes. These habits cause some distress but remain manageable within your daily routine.

responsibility.label: Moderate
responsibility.description: Responsibility-related obsessions and checking compulsions are moderately disrupting your daily functioning. You may repeatedly verify locks, appliances, or decisions, and seek excessive reassurance from others. ERP-based treatment can effectively address these patterns.

responsibility.label: Severe
responsibility.description: Severe harm-related obsessions and checking compulsions are causing major impairment in your daily life. You likely spend extensive time on repetitive checking, experience intense guilt or anxiety, and significantly avoid situations where you might be responsible for harm.

unacceptable_thoughts.label: Minimal
unacceptable_thoughts.description: You report no significant distress from unwanted intrusive thoughts. Occasional odd or unpleasant thoughts may occur but are easily dismissed without engaging in mental rituals or avoidance behaviors.

unacceptable_thoughts.label: Mild
unacceptable_thoughts.description: You experience mild distress from unwanted intrusive thoughts of a violent, sexual, or immoral nature. These thoughts may cause guilt or anxiety, but you can generally manage the discomfort without major disruption to daily life.

unacceptable_thoughts.label: Moderate
unacceptable_thoughts.description: Unacceptable intrusive thoughts are causing moderate distress and may lead to mental rituals, avoidance of triggering situations, or significant self-doubt about your character. Professional support can help you understand that these thoughts do not reflect your true intentions.

unacceptable_thoughts.label: Severe
unacceptable_thoughts.description: Severe distress from unwanted intrusive thoughts is significantly impairing your daily functioning and emotional well-being. You may engage in extensive mental rituals, avoid numerous situations, and experience intense shame or fear. Specialized OCD treatment is essential.

symmetry.label: Minimal
symmetry.description: You report no significant concerns about symmetry, order, or balance. Preferences for neatness or organization are within normal range and do not cause distress or consume excessive time.

symmetry.label: Mild
symmetry.description: You experience a mild need for symmetry, exactness, or particular arrangements. Some extra time may be spent ordering or arranging items, but these behaviors do not substantially interfere with your productivity or relationships.

symmetry.label: Moderate
symmetry.description: Symmetry and ordering concerns are moderately affecting your daily life, potentially consuming significant time and causing distress when things feel unbalanced or incomplete. Tasks may take much longer due to the need to achieve a particular arrangement.

symmetry.label: Severe
symmetry.description: Severe symmetry obsessions and ordering compulsions are dominating your daily routine and causing major functional impairment. You may spend hours arranging, counting, or repeating actions until they feel just right. Specialized treatment is strongly recommended.
